product_updates.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
product_updates.content: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

product_updates.title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
product_updates.content: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

product_updates.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
product_updates.content: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

product_updates.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
product_updates.content: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

product_updates.title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
product_updates.content: When you edit your Taste Profile, you'll impact your personalized playlists like Discover Weekly, recommendations, and Wrapped.

product_updates.title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
product_updates.content: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the largest venture-backed acquisition in history — Google’s $32 billion deal, finalized after a declined 20…

product_updates.title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
product_updates.content: Peacock is betting on new AI-powered video experiences, vertical clips, and mobile games to help its growth.

product_updates.title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
product_updates.content: After selling his AI startup to AMD for $665 million, Peter Sarlin is back with Qutwo, a new venture building the infrastructure it believes enterprises will need when quantum computing finally arrives.

product_updates.title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
product_updates.content: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a call on their behalf if they suspect a family member might get scammed.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
technical_breakthroughs.content: Unicode that's invisible to the human eye was largely abandoned—until attackers took notice.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
technical_breakthroughs.content: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s manufacturing leaders face a different challenge: how to grow amid labor constraints, rising complexity, an…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: how AI is used for military targeting, and the Pentagon’s war on Claude
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
technical_breakthroughs.content: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers.
